description: The Houthis are a Zaydi revivalist and Shia Islamist political and military organization that emerged from Yemen in the 1990s. Founded by Hussein al-Houthi, the group opposes the Yemeni government and is backed by Iran. It has been a central player in Yemen's civil war, launching attacks on Saudi Arabia, Israel, and Red Sea shipping. The movement is designated as a terrorist organization by several countries.

country_links.evidence_quote: Chinese state-owned Chang Guang Satellite Technology Corporation has provided geospatial intelligence to the Houthis to target U.S. warships in the Red Sea.
country_links.context: Chinese companies supply dual-use materials and components for Houthi missiles and drones, and China provides geospatial intelligence to target U.S. warships.

country_links.evidence_quote: Iran also anchored the MV Saviz intelligence vessel, disguised as a regular cargo vessel, off the coast of Eritrea, that provided intelligence and updates on Coalition ship movements to the Houthis.
country_links.context: Iran anchored an intelligence vessel off the coast of Eritrea to provide intelligence to the Houthis.

country_links.evidence_quote: the Houthis sent a group from Saada to Beijing to study Mandarin and manage the supply of drones and missile guidance systems from China and Hong Kong to Yemen.
country_links.context: The Houthis manage supply of drones and missile guidance systems from Hong Kong to Yemen.

country_links.evidence_quote: Iranian supreme leader Ali Khamenei announced his 'spiritual' support of the movement in a personal meeting with the Houthi spokesperson Mohammed Abdul Salam in Tehran...
country_links.context: The Houthis receive military support and direction from Iran, including meetings with Iranian leaders and weapons transfers.

country_links.evidence_quote: In September, the missile was fired at Israel and landed in an open area near Ben Gurion Airport...
country_links.context: The Houthis fired a Palestine-2 missile at Israel, landing near Ben Gurion Airport.

country_links.evidence_quote: a confidential United Nations investigation had found the North Korean government... was 'trying to sell weapons to Yemen's Houthis'.
country_links.context: North Korea attempted to sell weapons to the Houthis and supplied Scud missiles used by the Houthis.

country_links.evidence_quote: personnel of Russia's GRU were stationed in Houthi-controlled parts of Yemen to assist the militia's attacks on merchant ships.
country_links.context: Russia supplies the Houthis with advanced anti-ship missiles and geospatial intelligence, and GRU personnel assist Houthi attacks.

country_links.evidence_quote: These weapons enabled the Houthis to strike targets deep in Saudi Arabia, including the capital Riyadh.
country_links.context: The Houthis strike targets deep in Saudi Arabia, including the capital Riyadh, using ballistic missiles and drones.

country_links.evidence_quote: al Shabaab is provided with weapons and training while also receiving assistance in expanding the criminal enterprises that fund their operations.
country_links.context: The Houthis have a deepening relationship with Al-Shabaab, providing weapons and training and cooperating in smuggling operations.

country_links.evidence_quote: On 1 October 2016, it was able to hit the UAE Navy's HSV-2 Swift hybrid catamaran with a single C-801/C-802 AShM fired from a shore battery.
country_links.context: The Houthis attacked the UAE Navy's HSV-2 Swift with an anti-ship missile, causing severe damage.
